response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and some embedded background, but he is disqualified from this role on multiple hard requirements simultaneously: the resume lists no Japanese language proficiency whatsoever (native-level Japanese is mandatory), his C/C++ work is minimal and confined to brief stints rather than being a primary skill, and he has no demonstrated experience in network security domains such as MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 switching, or embedded firmware development. The gap between his profile and this role's core requirements is too large to bridge at interview.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development across web, mobile, and embedded contexts, well exceeding the minimum 3-year threshold
Demonstrated Linux experience and low-level systems work (C/C++ at National Instruments, Sound Position Server thesis, DLang async I/O at Sociomantic)
Strong collaborative and cross-functional track record — TDD, CI/CD, team teaching, open-source maintainer
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed anywhere on the resume — this alone is a hard disqualifier for the role
No security domain experience: zero evidence of MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption protocols, or network-attack detection
C programming and Linux kernel work are peripheral rather than central to the candidate's career; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang with no embedded networking or switch-chip experience
